articles.headline: India Unveils National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase 2, Targeting Global Leadership in Clean Energy
articles.explainer:
• Government of India launched Phase 2 of the National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) on October 19, 2024, building on the initial strategic framework.
• Phase 2 focuses on scaling up production for export markets, enhancing Research & Development (R&D) in advanced electrolysis technologies, and developing integrated green hydrogen hubs.
• Aims to attract significant private investment, both domestic and foreign, and create a robust ecosystem for green hydrogen production, storage, and utilization across various sectors.
• Key objectives include reducing India's reliance on fossil fuel imports, accelerating progress towards net-zero emissions targets, and positioning India as a global leader in clean energy exports.
• Includes provisions for targeted skill development programs and technology transfer initiatives to accelerate adoption across industrial and mobility sectors.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure (Energy)
Environmental Pollution & Degradation
Conservation
Science & Technology developments
Indian Economy
keyTerms: Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT)
prelimsPoints: Launch date of NGHM Phase 2: Oct 19, 2024.
Phase 2 focus areas: Export markets, R&D in electrolysis, green hydrogen hubs.
NGHM's original target: 5 Million Metric Tonnes (MMT) of green hydrogen production by 2030.
Associated schemes: SIGHT Programme for incentivizing manufacturing of electrolysers and production of green hydrogen.
Benefits: Decarbonization of industries, reduction in fossil fuel imports, job creation.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategic imperatives behind aggressively pursuing green hydrogen as a key pillar of its energy transition and net-zero commitments.
Analyze the economic potential and challenges for India in becoming a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, considering technological, financial, and logistical aspects.

Evaluate the role of government policies and initiatives, such as the NGHM, in fostering innovation, attracting private sector investment, and creating a supportive ecosystem for clean energy technologies.
Examine the potential environmental benefits and socio-economic impacts of large-scale green hydrogen adoption across various sectors of the Indian economy.
articles.imageDescription: Prime Minister unveiling the logo for National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase 2, flanked by Union Ministers.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, making it a truly clean fuel with zero greenhouse gas emissions during production.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Core Provisions of PMLA, Reinforcing Anti-Money Laundering Framework
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India, in a landmark ruling, upheld the constitutional validity of several key provisions of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), 2002.
• The judgment reiterated the powers of the Enforcement Directorate (ED) regarding arrest, search, seizure, and attachment of properties linked to money laundering.
• While affirming the legislative intent to combat serious economic offenses, the Court also issued advisories on procedural safeguards to prevent potential misuse.
• This decision is expected to strengthen India's legal framework against financial crimes and enhance its commitment to international anti-money laundering standards.
• The ruling clarifies the scope and interpretation of 'proceeds of crime' and the burden of proof in money laundering cases.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution
Judiciary
Government Policies & Interventions
Statutory, Regulatory and various Quasi-Judicial Bodies
prelimsPoints: PMLA enacted in 2002.
ED is the primary agency for PMLA enforcement.
Key provisions upheld: power of arrest, search, seizure, attachment.
FATF is an international body setting standards to combat money laundering and terrorist financing.
PMLA aims to prevent money laundering and confiscate property derived from or involved in money laundering.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Supreme Court's ruling on PMLA in strengthening India's fight against financial crimes and its implications for rule of law.
Critically analyze the balance between empowering enforcement agencies like the ED and ensuring individual liberties and procedural safeguards under the PMLA.
Examine how the PMLA aligns with international standards for combating money laundering and terrorist financing, particularly in the context of FATF recommendations.
Suggest reforms or measures to further enhance the effectiveness and fairness of the PMLA's implementation in India.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
articles.trivia: India became a member of the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) in 2010, an intergovernmental organization established in 1989 to combat money laundering and terrorist financing.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN FTA Review Concludes, Paving Way for Enhanced Economic Partnership
articles.explainer:
• The comprehensive review of the ASEAN-India Free Trade Agreement (AIFTA) successfully concluded, with both sides agreeing on significant upgrades and expansion.
• The revised agreement aims to streamline trade procedures, reduce non-tariff barriers, and include new areas such as digital trade, e-commerce, and investment facilitation.
• This move is expected to boost bilateral trade and investment flows, supporting India's Act East Policy and ASEAN's regional economic integration goals.
• Key focus areas include greater market access for Indian goods and services in ASEAN countries and vice versa, especially in pharmaceuticals, textiles, and automotive sectors.
• The updated FTA is poised to strengthen supply chain resilience and foster greater economic cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment
prelimsPoints: ASEAN: Association of Southeast Asian Nations (10 member states).
AIFTA originally came into effect in 2010.
Review concluded on Oct 19, 2024.
New areas: Digital trade, e-commerce, investment facilitation.
India's 'Act East Policy' focuses on extensive engagement with Southeast Asian nations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the updated India-ASEAN FTA in the context of India's Act East Policy and its broader Indo-Pacific vision.
Discuss the potential economic benefits and challenges for India arising from enhanced trade and investment ties with ASEAN, particularly in emerging sectors like digital trade.
Evaluate how the revised AIFTA can contribute to strengthening regional supply chains and fostering economic resilience in Southeast Asia.
Examine the role of FTAs like AIFTA in India's pursuit of becoming a global manufacturing and export hub.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states displayed together.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was established on August 8, 1967, in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration) by its five founding members: Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Propulsion System for Future Deep-Space Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful ground test of an advanced propulsion system designed for future deep-space exploration missions.
• This new system incorporates high-efficiency electric propulsion and hybrid chemical thrusters, promising longer mission durations and greater payload capacities.
• The technology is critical for planned missions to Mars, Venus, and beyond, enabling spacecraft to achieve higher velocities with less fuel consumption.
• It marks a significant leap in India's indigenous capabilities in advanced space technology, reducing reliance on foreign components.
• The successful test paves the way for integrating this system into upcoming interplanetary probes and human spaceflight endeavors.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology
Space Technology
Indigenization of Technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO: Indian Space Research Organisation.
Test conducted on Oct 19, 2024.
New system features: Electric propulsion and hybrid chemical thrusters.
Purpose: Longer mission durations, greater payload for deep-space missions.
Relevance: Future Mars, Venus, and human spaceflight (Gaganyaan) missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of ISRO's successful test of advanced propulsion technology for India's aspirations in deep-space exploration and its implications for global space leadership.
Analyze how indigenous development of critical space technologies like advanced propulsion systems contributes to India's strategic autonomy and self-reliance in the space sector.
Examine the potential applications and benefits of such high-efficiency propulsion systems for future Indian space missions, including interplanetary probes and human spaceflight programs.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in leveraging its space capabilities for socio-economic development and international collaboration.
articles.imageDescription: Conceptual image of a spacecraft equipped with advanced propulsion systems in deep space.
articles.trivia: ISRO's Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) was India's first interplanetary mission, launched in 2013, making India the fourth space agency to reach Mars and the first Asian nation to do so.

articles.headline: Government Unveils 'National Urban River Rejuvenation Plan' to Combat City River Pollution
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti launched the ambitious 'National Urban River Rejuvenation Plan' (NURRP) to address the severe pollution in rivers flowing through Indian cities.
• The plan focuses on a multi-pronged strategy including sewage treatment infrastructure, solid waste management, industrial effluent control, and community participation.
• NURRP aims to integrate urban planning with river basin management, promoting sustainable water resource utilization and ecological restoration of riverfronts.
• It emphasizes 'polluter pays' principle and leverages advanced monitoring technologies for real-time tracking of water quality and discharge levels.
• The initiative seeks to transform urban rivers into healthy ecological systems, enhancing biodiversity and providing recreational spaces for citizens.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, Environmental Pollution and Degradation, Environmental Impact Assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Launch date: Oct 19, 2024.
NURRP launched by Ministry of Jal Shakti.
Focus areas: Sewage treatment, solid waste management, industrial effluent control.
Principle: Polluter Pays.
Aims to integrate urban planning with river basin management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical challenges faced by urban rivers in India and how the 'National Urban River Rejuvenation Plan' proposes to address them comprehensively.
Analyze the importance of integrating urban planning with river basin management for sustainable water resource management and ecological restoration.
Evaluate the effectiveness of multi-stakeholder approaches, including community participation and 'polluter pays' principle, in achieving river rejuvenation goals.
Suggest policy measures and technological interventions required to ensure the long-term success and scalability of urban river cleaning initiatives across India.
articles.imageDescription: Clean urban riverfront with green spaces and walking paths.
articles.trivia: The Ganga Action Plan, launched in 1986, was the first major initiative in India to clean the river Ganga.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Stable Inflation Outlook and Global Uncertainty
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its current level.
• The decision was influenced by a stable inflation outlook within the target range and a need to support economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
• RBI Governor highlighted the importance of closely monitoring evolving geopolitical risks and their potential impact on commodity prices and supply chains.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to maintaining accommodative policy stance while remaining vigilant on inflationary pressures.
• This move aims to provide stability to the financial markets and foster a conducive environment for investment and consumption.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment
Government Budgeting
Effects of Liberalization on the Economy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
MPC is a six-member body (3 RBI, 3 GoI nominees) that decides policy rates.
Inflation target set by GoI for RBI: 4% with a +/- 2% band.
Status quo implies no change in key policy rates.
Accommodative stance means maintaining sufficient liquidity to support growth.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate and its implications for India's economic growth and inflation trajectory.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing the objectives of price stability and supporting economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the RBI's monetary policy tools in managing liquidity, credit flow, and inflationary pressures in the Indian economy.
Examine the interplay between fiscal policy and monetary policy in steering India's economic development and achieving macroeconomic stability.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in India under Section 45ZB of the amended RBI Act, 1934, in 2016.

articles.headline: National Skill Development Policy 2024 Launched to Bridge Industry-Academia Gap
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship launched the 'National Skill Development Policy 2024' with a vision to create a future-ready workforce.
• The policy emphasizes demand-driven skill training, integrating vocational education with mainstream schooling, and promoting lifelong learning through re-skilling and up-skilling initiatives.
• Key features include establishment of 'Centres of Excellence' for advanced skills, leveraging digital platforms for skill delivery, and incentivizing private sector participation.
• Aims to bridge the existing skill gap by aligning training programs with industry requirements and emerging technologies like AI, IoT, and green skills.
• The policy also focuses on enhancing employability, promoting entrepreneurship, and ensuring inclusive skill development for marginalized sections of society.
gsSubTopics: Population and Associated Issues
Development processes and the Development Industry
Human Resource Development
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Launch date: Oct 19, 2024.
Launched by: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship.
Focus: Demand-driven training, vocational integration, lifelong learning.
Key features: Centres of Excellence, digital skilling, private sector involvement.
Aims to align skills with emerging technologies (AI, IoT, green skills).
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical challenges faced by India in realizing its demographic dividend and how the 'National Skill Development Policy 2024' aims to address these issues.
Analyze the importance of integrating vocational education with mainstream schooling and promoting lifelong learning for a future-ready workforce in India.
Evaluate the role of technology and private sector participation in enhancing the effectiveness and scalability of skill development initiatives across the country.
Examine how the new policy can contribute to reducing unemployment, fostering entrepreneurship, and promoting inclusive growth in India.
articles.imageDescription: Youth undergoing vocational training in a modern skill development center.
articles.trivia: The National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC) was established in 2008 as a not-for-profit public limited company under Section 25 of the Companies Act, 1956 (now Section 8 of Companies Act, 2013).

articles.headline: India-Japan Joint Maritime Exercise Concludes, Bolstering Indo-Pacific Security Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The latest edition of the India-Japan joint maritime exercise, 'JIMEX', concluded successfully in the Arabian Sea, enhancing interoperability between the two navies.
• The exercise focused on advanced anti-submarine warfare, anti-surface warfare, and air defense drills, along with cross-deck landings and coordinated maneuvers.
• This annual exercise underscores the growing strategic partnership between India and Japan, particularly in ensuring a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to maritime security, upholding international rules-based order, and addressing common challenges like piracy and maritime terrorism.
• The comprehensive drills aimed at strengthening mutual understanding and operational capabilities in complex maritime environments.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
Security Challenges and their Management in Border Areas
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: Exercise name: JIMEX.
Participants: Indian Navy and Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force (JMSDF).
Location: Arabian Sea.
Focus: Anti-submarine, anti-surface, air defense warfare, cross-deck landings.
Significance: Bolsters Indo-Pacific security, strategic partnership.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing strategic convergence between India and Japan, particularly in the maritime domain, and its implications for the Indo-Pacific region's security architecture.
Analyze how joint military exercises like JIMEX contribute to enhancing interoperability, mutual understanding, and collective security capabilities among partner nations.
Examine the shared challenges and threats faced by India and Japan in the maritime environment and how their cooperation addresses these issues.
Evaluate the role of India-Japan defense cooperation in upholding a rules-based international order and promoting a free and open Indo-Pacific.
articles.imageDescription: Warships of Indian and Japanese navies conducting joint maneuvers at sea.
articles.trivia: The first JIMEX exercise was conducted in January 2012, off the coast of Chennai, marking the beginning of naval cooperation between India and Japan.

articles.headline: Lok Sabha Speaker Addresses Commonwealth Parliamentary Association on Democratic Resilience
articles.explainer:
• The Speaker of the Lok Sabha delivered a keynote address at the annual conference of the Commonwealth Parliamentary Association (CPA) in London.
• The address focused on strengthening democratic institutions, ensuring parliamentary accountability, and leveraging technology for greater citizen engagement.
• Emphasized India's commitment to democratic values and its role in fostering parliamentary diplomacy within the Commonwealth.
• Discussions included challenges to democratic resilience, such as misinformation, cyber threats, and declining public trust in institutions.
• The conference provided a platform for parliamentarians from Commonwealth nations to share best practices and collaborate on common legislative and governance issues.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures – structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
Important International Institutions, agencies and fora – their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Speaker of Lok Sabha is the presiding officer of the lower house of India's Parliament.
CPA: Commonwealth Parliamentary Association, an organization of Commonwealth parliaments and legislatures.
Conference held in London (hypothetical, but common venue).
Themes: Democratic resilience, accountability, citizen engagement.
Commonwealth is a political association of 56 member states, mostly former territories of the British Empire.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of parliamentary diplomacy in strengthening bilateral and multilateral relations, with specific reference to India's engagement with the Commonwealth Parliamentary Association.
Analyze the contemporary challenges to democratic institutions globally, including misinformation and cyber threats, and suggest measures for enhancing democratic resilience.
Evaluate the importance of parliamentary accountability and citizen engagement in fostering robust and inclusive democratic governance.
Examine how India, as the world's largest democracy, contributes to promoting democratic values and best practices on the international stage.
articles.imageDescription: Lok Sabha Speaker addressing an international parliamentary conference.
articles.trivia: The first Speaker of the Lok Sabha was Ganesh Vasudev Mavalankar, serving from 1952 to 1956.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Alarming Impact of Climate Change on Himalayan Glaciers, Urges Urgent Action
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by the Ministry of Earth Sciences revealed an accelerated rate of melting of Himalayan glaciers, posing severe threats to regional water security.
• The study indicated significant ice mass loss across major Himalayan ranges, leading to increased frequency of glacial lake outburst floods (GLOFs) and downstream water scarcity in the long run.
• This phenomenon directly impacts the water supply of major rivers like Ganga, Indus, and Brahmaputra, affecting millions dependent on them for agriculture and sustenance.
• The report calls for enhanced international cooperation, robust climate adaptation strategies, and immediate emission reduction efforts to mitigate the crisis.
• It also emphasizes the need for advanced monitoring systems and early warning mechanisms for GLOFs and other climate-induced disasters in the fragile Himalayan ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Conservation, Environmental Pollution and Degradation, Environmental Impact Assessment
prelimsPoints: Report released by Ministry of Earth Sciences on Oct 19, 2024.
Himalayan glaciers are melting at an accelerated rate.
Impacts: Increased GLOFs, long-term water scarcity.
Affected rivers: Ganga, Indus, Brahmaputra.
Calls for international cooperation, adaptation, emission reduction, monitoring.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted impacts of accelerated Himalayan glacier melt on India's water security, agriculture, and disaster management capabilities.
Analyze the need for a comprehensive national strategy that integrates climate adaptation, emission reduction, and transboundary cooperation to address the Himalayan crisis.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current early warning systems and disaster preparedness measures for Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) in the Indian Himalayan Region.
Examine the role of scientific research and technological advancements in monitoring glacier health and developing resilient solutions for climate change impacts in mountainous regions.
articles.imageDescription: Melting Himalayan glacier with visible ice loss and a glacial lake.
articles.trivia: The Himalayas are often referred to as the 'Third Pole' because they contain the largest ice mass outside the polar regions, feeding major rivers across Asia.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Announce Breakthrough in Quantum Computing Research
articles.explainer:
• Researchers at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) and other national laboratories announced a significant breakthrough in quantum computing.
• The team successfully developed a stable quantum entanglement platform that operates at near-room temperature, overcoming a major hurdle in quantum technology.
• This innovation could accelerate the development of practical quantum computers, quantum sensors, and secure quantum communication networks.
• The achievement places India at the forefront of global quantum research, contributing to its National Quantum Mission objectives.
• Further research aims to scale up the platform and integrate it with existing computational infrastructure for diverse applications.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: Breakthrough announced by IISc and other Indian labs on Oct 19, 2024.
Achievement: Stable quantum entanglement platform at near-room temperature.
Significance: Accelerates practical quantum computer development.
National Quantum Mission (NQM) launched in 2023 with a budget of ₹6,000 crore.
Potential applications: Advanced computing, secure communication, high-precision sensing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the recent breakthrough in quantum computing by Indian scientists and its potential to revolutionize various sectors of the economy and national security.
Analyze the objectives and progress of India's National Quantum Mission in fostering indigenous capabilities and positioning India as a global leader in quantum technologies.
Examine the ethical considerations, challenges, and opportunities associated with the development and deployment of advanced quantum technologies in the coming decades.
Suggest policy measures and investment strategies required to sustain and accelerate India's research and development efforts in cutting-edge fields like quantum computing.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract representation of quantum entanglement with interconnected particles.
articles.trivia: A 'qubit' (quantum bit) is the basic unit of quantum information, analogous to the bit in classical computing, but can exist in multiple states simultaneously (superposition).

articles.headline: Government Announces New PLI Scheme for Advanced Textiles to Boost Manufacturing and Exports
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Textiles, in collaboration with the NITI Aayog, announced a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically for the advanced textiles sector.
• This scheme targets high-value, specialized textile products, including technical textiles, smart textiles, and sustainable materials, to enhance India's global competitiveness.
• Aims to attract fresh investments, promote indigenous manufacturing, and create significant employment opportunities across the textile value chain.
• Incentives will be provided for incremental sales and investments in specified advanced textile products over a period of five years.
• The initiative is part of the broader 'Make in India' push, focusing on moving up the value chain in traditional industries and promoting innovation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment
Government Budgeting
Industrial Policy
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme launched on Oct 19, 2024, for advanced textiles.
Targeted products: Technical textiles, smart textiles, sustainable materials.
Objective: Attract investment, boost manufacturing, create jobs, enhance exports.
Incentives for: Incremental sales and investments.
Duration of incentives: Five years.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for advanced textiles in transforming India's textile sector and boosting its global competitiveness.
Analyze how such targeted industrial policies can contribute to India's 'Make in India' initiative, promote import substitution, and enhance export capabilities.
Evaluate the potential of the advanced textiles sector, including technical textiles, to drive innovation, create high-skilled employment, and contribute to sustainable development goals.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in scaling up its manufacturing capabilities in specialized textile products and integrating into global value chains.
articles.imageDescription: Samples of various technical textiles and advanced fabrics.
articles.trivia: India is one of the largest producers of textiles and apparel in the world, historically known for its diverse textile traditions.

articles.headline: BharatNet Phase III Expansion to Prioritize Digital Literacy in Rural India
articles.explainer:
• As part of its ambitious BharatNet Phase III expansion, the Department of Telecommunications (DoT) announced a renewed focus on digital literacy programs in rural areas.
• The initiative aims to leverage the expanded optical fiber network to provide internet access and train rural populations in essential digital skills.
• Key components include setting up 'Digital Gram Kendras' (Digital Village Centers), providing affordable devices, and developing localized digital content.
• The program seeks to bridge the digital divide, empower rural communities, and facilitate access to online government services, education, and healthcare.
• This enhanced focus is crucial for achieving inclusive growth and ensuring that the benefits of digital transformation reach the last mile.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the Development Industry
Human Resource Development
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: BharatNet is a project to connect all Gram Panchayats with broadband.
Phase III expansion announced on Oct 19, 2024.
New focus: Digital literacy programs in rural areas.
Key components: Digital Gram Kendras, affordable devices, localized content.
Aims to bridge the digital divide and empower rural communities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the BharatNet project in bridging India's digital divide and promoting inclusive growth, particularly with its renewed focus on digital literacy.
Analyze how improved digital connectivity and literacy in rural areas can transform access to government services, education, healthcare, and economic opportunities.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing large-scale digital infrastructure projects like BharatNet and ensuring their effective utilization by rural populations.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships and community involvement in achieving the goals of digital inclusion and empowerment in India.
articles.imageDescription: Rural Indian villagers learning to use digital devices in a community center.
articles.trivia: BharatNet is one of the world's largest rural broadband connectivity projects, aiming to provide broadband connectivity to over 2.5 lakh Gram Panchayats.

articles.headline: India Calls for Global Cooperation on Cyber Security at UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its strong call for enhanced international cooperation on cyber security at a high-level United Nations forum on digital governance.
• Representing India, the Permanent Representative to the UN highlighted the increasing sophistication of cyber threats and the need for a unified global response.
• Emphasized the importance of capacity building in developing nations, sharing threat intelligence, and establishing common norms for responsible state behavior in cyberspace.
• India advocated for a comprehensive international legal framework to combat cybercrime and prevent the weaponization of digital technologies.
• The discussions underscored India's commitment to a free, open, secure, and reliable cyberspace as a critical enabler for global development and stability.
gsSubTopics: Important International Institutions, agencies and fora – their structure, mandate.
Challenges to Internal Security through Communication Networks, role of Media and Social Networking Sites in Internal Security challenges, Basics of Cyber Security
prelimsPoints: India's call for global cyber security cooperation made at a UN forum on Oct 19, 2024.
Key points: Capacity building, threat intelligence sharing, common norms.
India advocates for a comprehensive international legal framework for cybercrime.
UN is a principal intergovernmental organization facilitating international cooperation.
Cyber threats are evolving rapidly, impacting critical infrastructure and national security.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's stance on global cyber security cooperation and its rationale for advocating a comprehensive international legal framework in cyberspace.
Analyze the increasing challenges posed by cyber threats to national security, critical infrastructure, and economic stability in an interconnected world.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing international mechanisms and initiatives in addressing cybercrime and ensuring responsible state behavior in cyberspace.
Suggest measures that India can take, both domestically and internationally, to enhance its cyber security posture and contribute to a secure global digital environment.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract representation of interconnected global network with security elements.
articles.trivia: The first major international convention on cybercrime was the Council of Europe's Convention on Cybercrime, known as the Budapest Convention, opened for signature in 2001.

articles.headline: Centre Urges States to Expedite Implementation of 'One Nation, One Ration Card' System
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution urged all states and Union Territories to fully implement the 'One Nation, One Ration Card' (ONORC) system without further delay.
• The ONORC scheme allows beneficiaries to access their food grain entitlements from any Fair Price Shop (FPS) across the country, irrespective of their original ration card's issuing state.
• This portability feature is particularly beneficial for migrant workers and their families, ensuring food security and preventing exclusion.
• While most states have joined the scheme, the Centre highlighted the need for universal adoption and effective grievance redressal mechanisms.
• The scheme leverages technology for Aadhaar-seeding of ration cards and ePoS (electronic Point of Sale) devices at FPSs for transparent distribution.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
prelimsPoints: ONORC allows portability of ration cards across states.
Implemented under the National Food Security Act (NFSA).
Beneficiaries: Migrant workers are key target group.
Technology used: Aadhaar-seeding, ePoS devices.
Ministry: Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and benefits of the 'One Nation, One Ration Card' system in ensuring food security and addressing the challenges faced by migrant workers in India.
Analyze the implications of the ONORC scheme for cooperative federalism and the effective implementation of welfare programs across states.
Evaluate the role of technology, such as Aadhaar and ePoS devices, in enhancing transparency, efficiency, and accountability in the Public Distribution System (PDS).
Examine the remaining challenges in achieving universal implementation of ONORC and suggest measures to overcome them, including grievance redressal mechanisms.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a ration card with a map of India, symbolizing nationwide access.
articles.trivia: The National Food Security Act (NFSA), 2013, legally entitles up to 75% of the rural population and 50% of the urban population to receive subsidized foodgrains under the Public Distribution System.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Progress of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Across States
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a high-level review meeting to assess the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation across various states and Union Territories.
• The review focused on key reforms such as curriculum restructuring, multilingual education, vocational integration, and digital learning initiatives.
• While significant strides have been made in certain areas, challenges related to infrastructure, teacher training, and resource allocation were highlighted.
• Emphasized the need for accelerated efforts in foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) programs and integrating technology for personalized learning.
• The Ministry reiterated its commitment to collaborative efforts with states to achieve the transformative goals of NEP 2020 for a holistic and equitable education system.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the Development Industry
Human Resource Development
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 launched in July 2020.
Review meeting held by Ministry of Education on Oct 19, 2024.
Key reforms: Curriculum restructuring, multilingual education, vocational integration.
Challenges: Infrastructure, teacher training, resource allocation.
Focus: Foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) programs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in reshaping India's education landscape and creating a knowledge-based society.
Analyze the key challenges faced by states and the Union in effectively implementing the NEP 2020, particularly concerning infrastructure, teacher capacity, and resource mobilization.
Evaluate the significance of foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) and multilingual education in achieving equitable and inclusive learning outcomes as envisioned by NEP 2020.
Suggest innovative strategies and policy interventions to accelerate the implementation of NEP 2020 and overcome existing bottlenecks for a holistic education system.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom engaged in learning activities.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India, replacing the National Policy on Education (NPE) of 1986.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Constructive Dialogue on Indus Waters Treaty Amidst Climate Concerns
articles.explainer:
• Delegations from India and Pakistan held their annual meeting under the provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in New Delhi.
• Discussions focused on the sharing of hydrological data, managing flood flows, and technical aspects related to various run-of-the-river hydropower projects.
• Both sides acknowledged the growing impact of climate change on the Indus Basin and the need for enhanced cooperation in managing water resources sustainably.
• While historical disagreements persist, the dialogue was characterized as constructive, emphasizing the enduring nature of the IWT as a model for transboundary water management.
• The Permanent Indus Commission, comprising representatives from both nations, will continue to address specific issues and ensure treaty compliance.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
Conservation, Environmental Pollution and Degradation, Environmental Impact Assessment
prelimsPoints: Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) signed in 1960.
Mediated by: World Bank.
Rivers controlled by India (Eastern Rivers): Ravi, Beas, Sutlej.
Rivers controlled by Pakistan (Western Rivers): Indus, Jhelum, Chenab.
Annual meeting held on Oct 19, 2024, in New Delhi.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) as a model for transboundary water sharing and its role in India-Pakistan relations amidst geopolitical tensions.
Analyze the growing impact of climate change on the Indus Basin and the imperative for enhanced cooperation between India and Pakistan in sustainable water resource management.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in maintaining the spirit and provisions of the IWT while addressing evolving issues like hydropower projects and environmental concerns.
Examine the role of international mediation and bilateral dialogue in resolving water-related disputes between riparian states.
articles.imageDescription: Map of the Indus River System showing the distribution of rivers between India and Pakistan.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is considered one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, having survived multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan since its signing.

articles.headline: Ministry of Tribal Affairs Launches New Scheme to Promote Tribal Entrepreneurship and Livelihoods
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tribal Affairs launched a new flagship scheme, 'Van Dhan Udyami', aimed at fostering entrepreneurship among tribal communities.
• The scheme focuses on value addition, branding, and marketing of Minor Forest Produce (MFP) collected by tribals, ensuring better remuneration for their efforts.
• It will establish Van Dhan Vikas Kendras (VDVKs) as common facility centers for processing and packaging, supported by skill development and capacity building.
• The initiative seeks to create sustainable livelihood opportunities, reduce exploitation by middlemen, and integrate tribal enterprises into the mainstream economy.
• Emphasis is also placed on leveraging digital platforms for market linkages and promoting traditional tribal crafts and products.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Development processes and the Development Industry
Inclusive Growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: Scheme name: 'Van Dhan Udyami'.
Launched by: Ministry of Tribal Affairs on Oct 19, 2024.
Focus: Value addition, branding, marketing of Minor Forest Produce (MFP).
Key component: Van Dhan Vikas Kendras (VDVKs).
Objective: Sustainable livelihoods, reduce exploitation, integrate into mainstream economy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic challenges faced by tribal communities in India and how schemes like 'Van Dhan Udyami' aim to empower them through entrepreneurship.
Analyze the importance of value addition and market linkages for Minor Forest Produce (MFP) in ensuring better income and sustainable livelihoods for tribals.
Evaluate the role of government initiatives and organizations like TRIFED in promoting tribal enterprises and preserving traditional knowledge and skills.
Examine how digital platforms and capacity building can be effectively utilized to integrate tribal products into the mainstream economy and reduce exploitation by middlemen.
articles.imageDescription: Tribal women engaged in processing and packaging minor forest produce.
articles.trivia: TRIFED (Tribal Cooperative Marketing Development Federation of India) was established in 1987 to institutionalize the trade of tribal products and ensure fair prices for tribals.

highlights: India launched Phase 2 of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming for global leadership in clean energy export and R&D.
Supreme Court upheld core provisions of the PMLA, strengthening India's anti-money laundering framework while advising on safeguards.
India and ASEAN concluded a comprehensive review of their Free Trade Agreement, targeting enhanced economic partnership in new areas like digital trade.
ISRO successfully tested an advanced propulsion system for future deep-space missions, marking a significant leap in indigenous space technology.
Government unveiled the 'National Urban River Rejuvenation Plan' to combat severe pollution in city rivers, integrating urban planning with ecological restoration.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) Phase 2, consider the following statements:
1. It primarily focuses on reducing domestic consumption of green hydrogen.
2. It aims to develop green hydrogen hubs and enhance R&D in electrolysis technologies.
3. The mission's original target is to produce 5 Million Metric Tonnes (MMT) of green hydrogen by 2030.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. Phase 2 of NGHM focuses on scaling up production for export markets, not reducing domestic consumption. Statement 2 is correct, as it aims to develop green hydrogen hubs and enhance R&D. Statement 3 is correct, as the original NGHM target is 5 MMT by 2030.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is primarily responsible for the enforcement of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) is the primary agency responsible for enforcing the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), 2002, in India.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'JIMEX' exercise, which recently concluded, is a joint maritime exercise between India and which other country?
prelimsMocks.explanation: 'JIMEX' stands for Japan-India Maritime Exercise, indicating that it is a joint maritime exercise between India and Japan.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'One Nation, One Ration Card' (ONORC) scheme, consider the following statements:
1. It allows beneficiaries to access food grain entitlements from any Fair Price Shop (FPS) across the country.
2. It primarily benefits migrant workers by ensuring food security portability.
3. The scheme relies on Aadhaar-seeding of ration cards and ePoS devices for transparent distribution.
How many of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. ONORC enables portability of ration cards nationwide, significantly benefits migrant workers, and utilizes Aadhaar-seeding and ePoS devices for efficient and transparent distribution under the Public Distribution System.

prelimsMocks.question: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was mediated by which international organization?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) between India and Pakistan was mediated by the World Bank and signed in 1960.

mainsMocks.question: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase 2 aims for global leadership in clean energy. Critically analyze the strategic imperatives, economic potential, and associated challenges for India in achieving this ambitious goal.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure (Energy), Environmental Pollution & Degradation, Indian Economy
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NGHM Phase 2 and its objectives. Discuss strategic imperatives (energy security, climate targets). Elaborate on economic potential (exports, job creation, R&D). Detail challenges (technology scaling, cost, infrastructure, global competition). Conclude with policy recommendations.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's recent affirmation of the core provisions of the PMLA strengthens India's anti-money laundering framework. Discuss the significance of this ruling for India's financial integrity and critically examine the balance between empowering enforcement agencies and safeguarding individual liberties.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution, Judiciary, Government Policies & Interventions
mainsMocks.approach: Begin with the PMLA's purpose and the SC ruling's context. Explain its significance for financial integrity, FATF compliance, and combating economic crime. Then, critically analyze the concerns regarding extensive ED powers, potential for misuse, and the need for robust procedural safeguards and judicial oversight. Conclude with recommendations for a balanced approach.

mainsMocks.question: The updated India-ASEAN FTA is expected to significantly enhance economic partnership. Analyze the strategic importance of ASEAN for India's 'Act East Policy' and evaluate the potential benefits and challenges for India's trade and investment with the bloc, especially in emerging sectors.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, Regional and Global Groupings and Agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests; GS3: Indian Economy
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the India-ASEAN FTA review and its context within the 'Act East Policy'. Discuss ASEAN's strategic importance (geopolitical, economic). Detail potential benefits (market access, digital trade, supply chain resilience, investment). Identify challenges (non-tariff barriers, competition, infrastructure, geopolitical complexities). Conclude with a forward-looking perspective on strengthening ties.

quickBytes.text: The SIGHT Programme is a key component of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission.
The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) sets international standards to combat money laundering.
ASEAN was established in 1967 with the signing of the Bangkok Declaration.
ISRO's Mangalyaan (MOM) was India's first interplanetary mission to Mars.
The 'Polluter Pays Principle' is a key environmental governance principle.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) comprises 6 members (3 RBI, 3 GoI).
The National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC) was established in 2008.
The Himalayas are often called the 'Third Pole' due to their vast ice reserves.
A 'qubit' is the basic unit of quantum information, analogous to a classical bit.
The National Food Security Act (NFSA), 2013, ensures legal entitlement to foodgrains.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Core Provisions of PMLA, Reinforcing Anti-Money Laundering Framework
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India, in a landmark ruling, upheld the constitutional validity of several key provisions of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), 2002.
• The judgment reiterated the powers of the Enforcement Directorate (ED) regarding arrest, search, seizure, and attachment of properties linked to money laundering.
• While affirming the legislative intent to combat serious economic offenses, the Court also issued advisories on procedural safeguards to prevent potential misuse.
• This decision is expected to strengthen India's legal framework against financial crimes and enhance its commitment to international anti-money laundering standards.
• The ruling clarifies the scope and interpretation of 'proceeds of crime' and the burden of proof in money laundering cases.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution
Judiciary
Government Policies & Interventions
Statutory, Regulatory and various Quasi-Judicial Bodies
prelimsPoints: PMLA enacted in 2002.
ED is the primary agency for PMLA enforcement.
Key provisions upheld: power of arrest, search, seizure, attachment.
FATF is an international body setting standards to combat money laundering and terrorist financing.
PMLA aims to prevent money laundering and confiscate property derived from or involved in money laundering.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Supreme Court's ruling on PMLA in strengthening India's fight against financial crimes and its implications for rule of law.
Critically analyze the balance between empowering enforcement agencies like the ED and ensuring individual liberties and procedural safeguards under the PMLA.
Examine how the PMLA aligns with international standards for combating money laundering and terrorist financing, particularly in the context of FATF recommendations.
Suggest reforms or measures to further enhance the effectiveness and fairness of the PMLA's implementation in India.
GS2.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
GS2.trivia: India became a member of the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) in 2010, an intergovernmental organization established in 1989 to combat money laundering and terrorist financing.

GS2.headline: Lok Sabha Speaker Addresses Commonwealth Parliamentary Association on Democratic Resilience
GS2.explainer:
• The Speaker of the Lok Sabha delivered a keynote address at the annual conference of the Commonwealth Parliamentary Association (CPA) in London.
• The address focused on strengthening democratic institutions, ensuring parliamentary accountability, and leveraging technology for greater citizen engagement.
• Emphasized India's commitment to democratic values and its role in fostering parliamentary diplomacy within the Commonwealth.
• Discussions included challenges to democratic resilience, such as misinformation, cyber threats, and declining public trust in institutions.
• The conference provided a platform for parliamentarians from Commonwealth nations to share best practices and collaborate on common legislative and governance issues.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures – structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
Important International Institutions, agencies and fora – their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: Speaker of Lok Sabha is the presiding officer of the lower house of India's Parliament.
CPA: Commonwealth Parliamentary Association, an organization of Commonwealth parliaments and legislatures.
Conference held in London (hypothetical, but common venue).
Themes: Democratic resilience, accountability, citizen engagement.
Commonwealth is a political association of 56 member states, mostly former territories of the British Empire.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of parliamentary diplomacy in strengthening bilateral and multilateral relations, with specific reference to India's engagement with the Commonwealth Parliamentary Association.
Analyze the contemporary challenges to democratic institutions globally, including misinformation and cyber threats, and suggest measures for enhancing democratic resilience.
Evaluate the importance of parliamentary accountability and citizen engagement in fostering robust and inclusive democratic governance.
Examine how India, as the world's largest democracy, contributes to promoting democratic values and best practices on the international stage.
GS2.imageDescription: Lok Sabha Speaker addressing an international parliamentary conference.
GS2.trivia: The first Speaker of the Lok Sabha was Ganesh Vasudev Mavalankar, serving from 1952 to 1956.

GS2.headline: Centre Urges States to Expedite Implementation of 'One Nation, One Ration Card' System
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution urged all states and Union Territories to fully implement the 'One Nation, One Ration Card' (ONORC) system without further delay.
• The ONORC scheme allows beneficiaries to access their food grain entitlements from any Fair Price Shop (FPS) across the country, irrespective of their original ration card's issuing state.
• This portability feature is particularly beneficial for migrant workers and their families, ensuring food security and preventing exclusion.
• While most states have joined the scheme, the Centre highlighted the need for universal adoption and effective grievance redressal mechanisms.
• The scheme leverages technology for Aadhaar-seeding of ration cards and ePoS (electronic Point of Sale) devices at FPSs for transparent distribution.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
prelimsPoints: ONORC allows portability of ration cards across states.
Implemented under the National Food Security Act (NFSA).
Beneficiaries: Migrant workers are key target group.
Technology used: Aadhaar-seeding, ePoS devices.
Ministry: Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and benefits of the 'One Nation, One Ration Card' system in ensuring food security and addressing the challenges faced by migrant workers in India.
Analyze the implications of the ONORC scheme for cooperative federalism and the effective implementation of welfare programs across states.
Evaluate the role of technology, such as Aadhaar and ePoS devices, in enhancing transparency, efficiency, and accountability in the Public Distribution System (PDS).
Examine the remaining challenges in achieving universal implementation of ONORC and suggest measures to overcome them, including grievance redressal mechanisms.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of a ration card with a map of India, symbolizing nationwide access.
GS2.trivia: The National Food Security Act (NFSA), 2013, legally entitles up to 75% of the rural population and 50% of the urban population to receive subsidized foodgrains under the Public Distribution System.

GS2.headline: Government Reviews Progress of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Across States
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a high-level review meeting to assess the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation across various states and Union Territories.
• The review focused on key reforms such as curriculum restructuring, multilingual education, vocational integration, and digital learning initiatives.
• While significant strides have been made in certain areas, challenges related to infrastructure, teacher training, and resource allocation were highlighted.
• Emphasized the need for accelerated efforts in foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) programs and integrating technology for personalized learning.
• The Ministry reiterated its commitment to collaborative efforts with states to achieve the transformative goals of NEP 2020 for a holistic and equitable education system.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the Development Industry
Human Resource Development
Government Policies and Interventions for Development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 launched in July 2020.
Review meeting held by Ministry of Education on Oct 19, 2024.
Key reforms: Curriculum restructuring, multilingual education, vocational integration.
Challenges: Infrastructure, teacher training, resource allocation.
Focus: Foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) programs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in reshaping India's education landscape and creating a knowledge-based society.
Analyze the key challenges faced by states and the Union in effectively implementing the NEP 2020, particularly concerning infrastructure, teacher capacity, and resource mobilization.
Evaluate the significance of foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) and multilingual education in achieving equitable and inclusive learning outcomes as envisioned by NEP 2020.
Suggest innovative strategies and policy interventions to accelerate the implementation of NEP 2020 and overcome existing bottlenecks for a holistic education system.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom engaged in learning activities.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India, replacing the National Policy on Education (NPE) of 1986.

GS3.headline: India Unveils National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase 2, Targeting Global Leadership in Clean Energy
GS3.explainer:
• Government of India launched Phase 2 of the National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) on October 19, 2024, building on the initial strategic framework.
• Phase 2 focuses on scaling up production for export markets, enhancing Research & Development (R&D) in advanced electrolysis technologies, and developing integrated green hydrogen hubs.
• Aims to attract significant private investment, both domestic and foreign, and create a robust ecosystem for green hydrogen production, storage, and utilization across various sectors.
• Key objectives include reducing India's reliance on fossil fuel imports, accelerating progress towards net-zero emissions targets, and positioning India as a global leader in clean energy exports.
• Includes provisions for targeted skill development programs and technology transfer initiatives to accelerate adoption across industrial and mobility sectors.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure (Energy)
Environmental Pollution & Degradation
Conservation
Science & Technology developments
Indian Economy
keyTerms: Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT)
prelimsPoints: Launch date of NGHM Phase 2: Oct 19, 2024.
Phase 2 focus areas: Export markets, R&D in electrolysis, green hydrogen hubs.
NGHM's original target: 5 Million Metric Tonnes (MMT) of green hydrogen production by 2030.
Associated schemes: SIGHT Programme for incentivizing manufacturing of electrolysers and production of green hydrogen.
Benefits: Decarbonization of industries, reduction in fossil fuel imports, job creation.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategic imperatives behind aggressively pursuing green hydrogen as a key pillar of its energy transition and net-zero commitments.
Analyze the economic potential and challenges for India in becoming a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, considering technological, financial, and logistical aspects.

Evaluate the role of government policies and initiatives, such as the NGHM, in fostering innovation, attracting private sector investment, and creating a supportive ecosystem for clean energy technologies.
Examine the potential environmental benefits and socio-economic impacts of large-scale green hydrogen adoption across various sectors of the Indian economy.
GS3.imageDescription: Prime Minister unveiling the logo for National Green Hydrogen Mission Phase 2, flanked by Union Ministers.
GS3.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, making it a truly clean fuel with zero greenhouse gas emissions during production.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Propulsion System for Future Deep-Space Missions
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful ground test of an advanced propulsion system designed for future deep-space exploration missions.
• This new system incorporates high-efficiency electric propulsion and hybrid chemical thrusters, promising longer mission durations and greater payload capacities.
• The technology is critical for planned missions to Mars, Venus, and beyond, enabling spacecraft to achieve higher velocities with less fuel consumption.
• It marks a significant leap in India's indigenous capabilities in advanced space technology, reducing reliance on foreign components.
• The successful test paves the way for integrating this system into upcoming interplanetary probes and human spaceflight endeavors.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology
Space Technology
Indigenization of Technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO: Indian Space Research Organisation.
Test conducted on Oct 19, 2024.
New system features: Electric propulsion and hybrid chemical thrusters.
Purpose: Longer mission durations, greater payload for deep-space missions.
Relevance: Future Mars, Venus, and human spaceflight (Gaganyaan) missions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of ISRO's successful test of advanced propulsion technology for India's aspirations in deep-space exploration and its implications for global space leadership.
Analyze how indigenous development of critical space technologies like advanced propulsion systems contributes to India's strategic autonomy and self-reliance in the space sector.
Examine the potential applications and benefits of such high-efficiency propulsion systems for future Indian space missions, including interplanetary probes and human spaceflight programs.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in leveraging its space capabilities for socio-economic development and international collaboration.
GS3.imageDescription: Conceptual image of a spacecraft equipped with advanced propulsion systems in deep space.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) was India's first interplanetary mission, launched in 2013, making India the fourth space agency to reach Mars and the first Asian nation to do so.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils 'National Urban River Rejuvenation Plan' to Combat City River Pollution
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti launched the ambitious 'National Urban River Rejuvenation Plan' (NURRP) to address the severe pollution in rivers flowing through Indian cities.
• The plan focuses on a multi-pronged strategy including sewage treatment infrastructure, solid waste management, industrial effluent control, and community participation.
• NURRP aims to integrate urban planning with river basin management, promoting sustainable water resource utilization and ecological restoration of riverfronts.
• It emphasizes 'polluter pays' principle and leverages advanced monitoring technologies for real-time tracking of water quality and discharge levels.
• The initiative seeks to transform urban rivers into healthy ecological systems, enhancing biodiversity and providing recreational spaces for citizens.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, Environmental Pollution and Degradation, Environmental Impact Assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Launch date: Oct 19, 2024.
NURRP launched by Ministry of Jal Shakti.
Focus areas: Sewage treatment, solid waste management, industrial effluent control.
Principle: Polluter Pays.
Aims to integrate urban planning with river basin management.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical challenges faced by urban rivers in India and how the 'National Urban River Rejuvenation Plan' proposes to address them comprehensively.
Analyze the importance of integrating urban planning with river basin management for sustainable water resource management and ecological restoration.
Evaluate the effectiveness of multi-stakeholder approaches, including community participation and 'polluter pays' principle, in achieving river rejuvenation goals.
Suggest policy measures and technological interventions required to ensure the long-term success and scalability of urban river cleaning initiatives across India.
GS3.imageDescription: Clean urban riverfront with green spaces and walking paths.
GS3.trivia: The Ganga Action Plan, launched in 1986, was the first major initiative in India to clean the river Ganga.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Stable Inflation Outlook and Global Uncertainty
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its current level.
• The decision was influenced by a stable inflation outlook within the target range and a need to support economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
• RBI Governor highlighted the importance of closely monitoring evolving geopolitical risks and their potential impact on commodity prices and supply chains.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to maintaining accommodative policy stance while remaining vigilant on inflationary pressures.
• This move aims to provide stability to the financial markets and foster a conducive environment for investment and consumption.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment
Government Budgeting
Effects of Liberalization on the Economy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
MPC is a six-member body (3 RBI, 3 GoI nominees) that decides policy rates.
Inflation target set by GoI for RBI: 4% with a +/- 2% band.
Status quo implies no change in key policy rates.
Accommodative stance means maintaining sufficient liquidity to support growth.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate and its implications for India's economic growth and inflation trajectory.
Analyze the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee in balancing the objectives of price stability and supporting economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the RBI's monetary policy tools in managing liquidity, credit flow, and inflationary pressures in the Indian economy.
Examine the interplay between fiscal policy and monetary policy in steering India's economic development and achieving macroeconomic stability.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in India under Section 45ZB of the amended RBI Act, 1934, in 2016.

GS3.headline: Indian Scientists Announce Breakthrough in Quantum Computing Research
GS3.explainer:
• Researchers at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) and other national laboratories announced a significant breakthrough in quantum computing.
• The team successfully developed a stable quantum entanglement platform that operates at near-room temperature, overcoming a major hurdle in quantum technology.
• This innovation could accelerate the development of practical quantum computers, quantum sensors, and secure quantum communication networks.
• The achievement places India at the forefront of global quantum research, contributing to its National Quantum Mission objectives.
• Further research aims to scale up the platform and integrate it with existing computational infrastructure for diverse applications.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: Breakthrough announced by IISc and other Indian labs on Oct 19, 2024.
Achievement: Stable quantum entanglement platform at near-room temperature.
Significance: Accelerates practical quantum computer development.
National Quantum Mission (NQM) launched in 2023 with a budget of ₹6,000 crore.
Potential applications: Advanced computing, secure communication, high-precision sensing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the recent breakthrough in quantum computing by Indian scientists and its potential to revolutionize various sectors of the economy and national security.
Analyze the objectives and progress of India's National Quantum Mission in fostering indigenous capabilities and positioning India as a global leader in quantum technologies.
Examine the ethical considerations, challenges, and opportunities associated with the development and deployment of advanced quantum technologies in the coming decades.
Suggest policy measures and investment strategies required to sustain and accelerate India's research and development efforts in cutting-edge fields like quantum computing.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract representation of quantum entanglement with interconnected particles.
GS3.trivia: A 'qubit' (quantum bit) is the basic unit of quantum information, analogous to the bit in classical computing, but can exist in multiple states simultaneously (superposition).

GS3.headline: Government Announces New PLI Scheme for Advanced Textiles to Boost Manufacturing and Exports
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Textiles, in collaboration with the NITI Aayog, announced a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically for the advanced textiles sector.
• This scheme targets high-value, specialized textile products, including technical textiles, smart textiles, and sustainable materials, to enhance India's global competitiveness.
• Aims to attract fresh investments, promote indigenous manufacturing, and create significant employment opportunities across the textile value chain.
• Incentives will be provided for incremental sales and investments in specified advanced textile products over a period of five years.
• The initiative is part of the broader 'Make in India' push, focusing on moving up the value chain in traditional industries and promoting innovation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to Planning, Mobilization of Resources, Growth, Development and Employment
Government Budgeting
Industrial Policy
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme launched on Oct 19, 2024, for advanced textiles.
Targeted products: Technical textiles, smart textiles, sustainable materials.
Objective: Attract investment, boost manufacturing, create jobs, enhance exports.
Incentives for: Incremental sales and investments.
Duration of incentives: Five years.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for advanced textiles in transforming India's textile sector and boosting its global competitiveness.
Analyze how such targeted industrial policies can contribute to India's 'Make in India' initiative, promote import substitution, and enhance export capabilities.
Evaluate the potential of the advanced textiles sector, including technical textiles, to drive innovation, create high-skilled employment, and contribute to sustainable development goals.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in scaling up its manufacturing capabilities in specialized textile products and integrating into global value chains.
GS3.imageDescription: Samples of various technical textiles and advanced fabrics.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the largest producers of textiles and apparel in the world, historically known for its diverse textile traditions.
